Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The Results support control of melanin biosynthesis and repression of many hydrolytic enzyme genes involved in host tissue utilization during late pathogenesis.

Regulation_Mode.reason: RNA-seq/qRT-PCR support positive regulation of melanin pathway genes and negative regulation of many hydrolytic enzyme-coding genes.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: Brn2, Scd1, Brn1, AbPks7-associated expression, Cbh7, lipase, chymotrypsin, CutAb, glycoside hydrolases, pectate lyases, and pectin esterase are supported as differentially regulated genes, but calling them all direct primary targets is stronger than shown.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: The paper directly supports activation of Brn2/Scd1/Brn1 and repression of many hydrolytic enzyme genes during late infection; AbPks7 expression was also reduced.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Amr1 expression is low during early infection and induced later; it is also expressed in conidia during conidiogenesis. The exact signal is not defined, but the timing statement is supported.

Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation.reason: The paper explicitly speculates that high GFP transcript in the promoter-tagged mutant could be due to absence of feedback inhibition, while noting this is not definitive.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Amr1 is expressed in highly melanized conidia/aerial hyphae and induced late in infection around 88 hpi, with low expression earlier.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed contexts except pectin as a carbon source are directly tied to Amr1 data; pectin assays were performed on mutants. Overall the combined environment description is supported.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Melanin is stated to be concentrated in conidial cell walls and septa.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper explicitly frames loss of Amr1 as increased virulence but decreased UV/glucanase resistance and melanin, indicating a trade-off between virulence and environmental survival.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper emphasizes the unusual increased virulence phenotype and discusses adaptation as saprophyte/facultative parasite.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq during infection and qRT-PCR validation are explicitly reported, with GEO GSE36781 accession.
